verses.latin_vulgate: Habet argentum venarum suarum principia,  et auro locus est in quo conflatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י יֵ֣שׁ לַכֶּ֣סֶף מוֹצָ֑א וּ֝מָק֗וֹם לַזָּהָ֥ב יָזֹֽקּוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Silver hath beginnings of its veins, and gold hath a place wherein it is melted.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hopialla on hänen lähtemisensä, ja kullalla sia, jossa valetaan.
verses.king_james: Surely there is a vein for the silver, and a place for gold where they fine it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ferrum de terra tollitur,  et lapis solutus calore in æs vertitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּ֭רְזֶל מֵעָפָ֣ר יֻקָּ֑ח וְ֝אֶ֗בֶן יָצ֥וּק נְחוּשָֽׁה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Iron is taken out of the earth, and stone melted with heat is turned into brass.
verses.finnish_biblia: Rauta otetaan maasta, ja kivistä vaski valetaan.
verses.king_james: Iron is taken out of the earth, and brass is molten out of the stone.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tempus posuit tenebris,  et universorum finem ipse considerat :  lapidem quoque caliginis et umbram mortis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קֵ֤ץ ׀ שָׂ֤ם לַחֹ֗שֶׁךְ וּֽלְכׇל־תַּ֭כְלִית ה֣וּא חוֹקֵ֑ר אֶ֖בֶן אֹ֣פֶל וְצַלְמָֽוֶת׃
verses.douay_rheims: He hath set a time for darkness, and the end of all things he considereth, the stone also that is in the dark and the shadow of death.
verses.finnish_biblia: Pimeydelle asetti hän lopun, ja kaiken täydellisyyden hän tutki, niin myös kiven, joka paksussa pimeydessä on, ja kuoleman varjon.
verses.king_james: He setteth an end to darkness, and searcheth out all perfection: the stones of darkness, and the shadow of death.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dividit torrens a populo peregrinante  eos quos oblitus est pes egentis hominis, et invios.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פָּ֤רַץ נַ֨חַל ׀ מֵֽעִם־גָּ֗ר הַֽנִּשְׁכָּחִ֥ים מִנִּי־רָ֑גֶל דַּ֖לּוּ מֵאֱנ֣וֹשׁ נָֽעוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The flood divideth from the people that are on their journey, those whom the food of the needy man hath forgotten, and who cannot be come at.
verses.finnish_biblia: Siitä vuotaa senkaltainen osa, että ympäriasuvaiset ei taida siitä jalkaisin käydä ylitse: se tyhjetään ihmisiltä ja vuotaa pois.
verses.king_james: The flood breaketh out from the inhabitant; even the waters forgotten of the foot: they are dried up, they are gone away from men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Terra de qua oriebatur panis,  in loco suo igni subversa est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶ֗רֶץ מִמֶּ֥נָּה יֵֽצֵא־לָ֑חֶם וְ֝תַחְתֶּ֗יהָ נֶהְפַּ֥ךְ כְּמוֹ־אֵֽשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The land, out of which bread grew in its place, hath been overturned with fire.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vilja kasvaa maasta, jonka alla se muutetaan niinkuin tuulelta.
verses.king_james: As for the earth, out of it cometh bread: and under it is turned up as it were fire.

verses.latin_vulgate: Locus sapphiri lapides ejus,  et glebæ illius aurum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְקוֹם־סַפִּ֥יר אֲבָנֶ֑יהָ וְעַפְרֹ֖ת זָהָ֣ב לֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The stones of it are the place of sapphires, and the clods of it are gold.
verses.finnish_biblia: Saphiri löydetään muutamista paikoista, ja maan kokkareita, joissa kultaa on.
verses.king_james: The stones of it are the place of sapphires: and it hath dust of gold.

verses.latin_vulgate: Semitam ignoravit avis,  nec intuitus est eam oculus vulturis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נָ֭תִיב לֹא־יְדָ֣עוֹ עָ֑יִט וְלֹ֥א שְׁ֝זָפַ֗תּוּ עֵ֣ין אַיָּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: The bird hath not known the path, neither hath the eye of the vulture beheld it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sitä polkua ei ole yksikään lintu tuntenut, eikä variksen silmä nähnyt.
verses.king_james: There is a path which no fowl knoweth, and which the vulture’s eye hath not seen:

verses.latin_vulgate: Non calcaverunt eam filii institorum,  nec pertransivit per eam leæna.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹֽא־הִדְרִיכ֥וּהוּ בְנֵי־שָׁ֑חַץ לֹֽא־עָדָ֖ה עָלָ֣יו שָֽׁחַל׃
verses.douay_rheims: The children of the merchants have not trodden it, neither hath the lioness passed by it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ne ylpiät sikiät ei ole sitä sotkuneet, eikä jalopeura käynyt sen päällä.
verses.king_james: The lion’s whelps have not trodden it, nor the fierce lion passed by it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad silicem extendit manum suam :  subvertit a radicibus montes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּ֭חַלָּמִישׁ שָׁלַ֣ח יָד֑וֹ הָפַ֖ךְ מִשֹּׁ֣רֶשׁ הָרִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: He hath stretched forth his hand to the flint, he hath overturned mountains from the roots.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kädellä kallioon ruvetaan, ja vuoret ylösalaisin kukistetaan.
verses.king_james: He putteth forth his hand upon the rock; he overturneth the mountains by the roots.

verses.latin_vulgate: In petris rivos excidit,  et omne pretiosum vidit oculus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּ֭צּוּרוֹת יְאֹרִ֣ים בִּקֵּ֑עַ וְכׇל־יְ֝קָ֗ר רָאֲתָ֥ה עֵינֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the rocks he hath cut out rivers, and his eye hath seen every precious thing.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ojat lasketaan kallioista, ja kaikkein kalliimmatkin näkee silmä.
verses.king_james: He cutteth out rivers among the rocks; and his eye seeth every precious thing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Profunda quoque fluviorum scrutatus est,  et abscondita in lucem produxit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֭בְּכִי נְהָר֣וֹת חִבֵּ֑שׁ וְ֝תַעֲלֻמָ֗הּ יֹ֣צִא אֽוֹר׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: The depths also of rivers he hath searched, and hidden things he hath brought forth to light.
verses.finnish_biblia: Virrat estetään juoksemasta, ja ne ilmoitetaan, mitkä näissä peitetyt ovat.
verses.king_james: He bindeth the floods from overflowing; and the thing that is hid bringeth he forth to light.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sapientia vero ubi invenitur ?  et quis est locus intelligentiæ ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽ֭הַחׇכְמָה מֵאַ֣יִן תִּמָּצֵ֑א וְאֵ֥י זֶ֝֗ה מְק֣וֹם בִּינָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: But where is wisdom to be found, and where is the place of understanding?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kusta taito löydetään, ja kussa on ymmärryksen sia?
verses.king_james: But where shall wisdom be found? and where is the place of understanding?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nescit homo pretium ejus,  nec invenitur in terra suaviter viventium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־יָדַ֣ע אֱנ֣וֹשׁ עֶרְכָּ֑הּ וְלֹ֥א תִ֝מָּצֵ֗א בְּאֶ֣רֶץ הַחַיִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Man knoweth not the price thereof, neither is it found in the land of them that live in delights.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ei yksikään ihminen tiedä hänen siaansa, eikä löydeta elävien maasta.
verses.king_james: Man knoweth not the price thereof; neither is it found in the land of the living.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abyssus dicit : Non est in me,  et mare loquitur : Non est mecum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּה֣וֹם אָ֭מַר לֹ֣א בִי־הִ֑יא וְיָ֥ם אָ֝מַ֗ר אֵ֣ין עִמָּדִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: The depth saith: It is not in me: and the sea saith: It is not with me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Syvyys sanoo: ei se ole minussa, ja meri sanoo: ei se ole minun tykönäni.
verses.king_james: The depth saith, It is not in me: and the sea saith, It is not with me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non dabitur aurum obrizum pro ea,  nec appendetur argentum in commutatione ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־יֻתַּ֣ן סְג֣וֹר תַּחְתֶּ֑יהָ וְלֹ֥א יִ֝שָּׁקֵ֗ל כֶּ֣סֶף מְחִירָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The finest gold shall not purchase it, neither shall silver be weighed in exchange for it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ei hänestä anneta kultaa, eikä hopiaa punnita sen hinnaksi.
verses.king_james: It cannot be gotten for gold, neither shall silver be weighed for the price thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non conferetur tinctis Indiæ coloribus,  nec lapidi sardonycho pretiosissimo vel sapphiro.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־תְ֭סֻלֶּה בְּכֶ֣תֶם אוֹפִ֑יר בְּשֹׁ֖הַם יָקָ֣ר וְסַפִּֽיר׃
verses.douay_rheims: It shall not be compared with the dyed colours of India, or with the most precious stone sardonyx, or the sapphire.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ei Ophirin kulta, eli kalliit Onikin ja Saphirin kivet ole verratut hänen kanssansa.
verses.king_james: It cannot be valued with the gold of Ophir, with the precious onyx, or the sapphire.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non adæquabitur ei aurum vel vitrum,  nec commutabuntur pro ea vasa auri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־יַעַרְכֶ֣נָּה זָ֭הָב וּזְכוֹכִ֑ית וּתְמ֖וּרָתָ֣הּ כְּלִי־פָֽז׃
verses.douay_rheims: Gold or crystal cannot equal it, neither shall any vessels of gold be changed for it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kulta ja kallis kivi ei taideta siihen verrattaa, eikä se taideta vaihetettaa kultaisiin astioihin.
verses.king_james: The gold and the crystal cannot equal it: and the exchange of it shall not be for jewels of fine gold.

verses.latin_vulgate: Excelsa et eminentia non memorabuntur comparatione ejus :  trahitur autem sapientia de occultis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רָאמ֣וֹת וְ֭גָבִישׁ לֹ֣א יִזָּכֵ֑ר וּמֶ֥שֶׁךְ חׇ֝כְמָ֗ה מִפְּנִינִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: High and eminent things shall not be mentioned in comparison of it: but wisdom is drawn out of secret places.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ramot ja Gabis ei ole mitään sen suhteen: se pidetään kalliimpana päärlyjä.
verses.king_james: No mention shall be made of coral, or of pearls: for the price of wisdom is above rubies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non adæquabitur ei topazius de Æthiopia,  nec tincturæ mundissimæ componetur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹֽא־יַ֭עַרְכֶנָּה פִּטְדַת־כּ֑וּשׁ בְּכֶ֥תֶם טָ֝ה֗וֹר לֹ֣א תְסֻלֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: The topaz of Ethiopia shall not be equal to it, neither shall it be compared to the cleanest dyeing.
verses.finnish_biblia: Topatsi Etiopiasta ei ole siihen verrattava, ja puhtain kulta ei maksa mitään sen suhteen.
verses.king_james: The topaz of Ethiopia shall not equal it, neither shall it be valued with pure gold.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unde ergo sapientia venit ?  et quis est locus intelligentiæ ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽ֭הַחׇכְמָה מֵאַ֣יִן תָּב֑וֹא וְאֵ֥י זֶ֝֗ה מְק֣וֹם בִּינָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Whence then cometh wisdom? and where is the place of understanding?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kusta siis taito tulee? ja kussa on ymmärryksen sia?
verses.king_james: Whence then cometh wisdom? and where is the place of understanding?

verses.latin_vulgate: Abscondita est ab oculis omnium viventium :  volucres quoque cæli latet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽ֭נֶעֶלְמָה מֵעֵינֵ֣י כׇל־חָ֑י וּמֵע֖וֹף הַשָּׁמַ֣יִם נִסְתָּֽרָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: It is hid from the eyes of all living, and the fowls of the air know it not.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on peitetty kaikkein elävien silmistä, ja salattu taivaan linnuiltakin.
verses.king_james: Seeing it is hid from the eyes of all living, and kept close from the fowls of the air.

verses.latin_vulgate: Perditio et mors dixerunt :  Auribus nostris audivimus famam ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲבַדּ֣וֹן וָ֭מָוֶת אָ֣מְר֑וּ בְּ֝אׇזְנֵ֗ינוּ שָׁמַ֥עְנוּ שִׁמְעָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Destruction and death have said: With our ears we have heard the fame thereof.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kadotus ja kuolema sanovat: me olemme korvillamme kuulleet hänen sanomansa.
verses.king_james: Destruction and death say, We have heard the fame thereof with our ears.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus intelligit viam ejus,  et ipse novit locum illius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱ֭לֹהִים הֵבִ֣ין דַּרְכָּ֑הּ וְ֝ה֗וּא יָדַ֥ע אֶת־מְקוֹמָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: God understandeth the way of it, and he knoweth the place thereof.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jumala tietää hänen tiensä, ja hän tuntee hänen siansa.
verses.king_james: God understandeth the way thereof, and he knoweth the place thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipse enim fines mundi intuetur,  et omnia quæ sub cælo sunt respicit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־ה֭וּא לִקְצוֹת־הָאָ֣רֶץ יַבִּ֑יט תַּ֖חַת כׇּל־הַשָּׁמַ֣יִם יִרְאֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: For he beholdeth the ends of the world: and looketh on all things that are under heaven.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä hän katselee maan ääriin, ja näkee kaikki, mitä taivaan alla on,
verses.king_james: For he looketh to the ends of the earth, and seeth under the whole heaven;

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui fecit ventis pondus,  et aquas appendit in mensura.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לַעֲשׂ֣וֹת לָר֣וּחַ מִשְׁקָ֑ל וּ֝מַ֗יִם תִּכֵּ֥ן בְּמִדָּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who made a weight for the winds, and weighed the waters by measure.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin että hän antaa tuulelle hänen painonsa ja vedelle hänen mittansa.
verses.king_james: To make the weight for the winds; and he weigheth the waters by measure.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quando ponebat pluviis legem,  et viam procellis sonantibus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּעֲשֹׂת֣וֹ לַמָּטָ֣ר חֹ֑ק וְ֝דֶ֗רֶךְ לַחֲזִ֥יז קֹלֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: When he gave a law for the rain, and a way for the sounding storms.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska hän asetti sateelle määrän ja pitkäisen tulen leimauksille hänen tiensä,
verses.king_james: When he made a decree for the rain, and a way for the lightning of the thunder:

verses.latin_vulgate: tunc vidit illam et enarravit,  et præparavit, et investigavit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֣ז רָ֭אָהּ וַֽיְסַפְּרָ֑הּ הֱ֝כִינָ֗הּ וְגַם־חֲקָרָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then he saw it, and declared, and prepared, and searched it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin näki hän sen ja luki sen: hän valmisti sen ja myös tutki sen,
verses.king_james: Then did he see it, and declare it; he prepared it, yea, and searched it out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit homini : Ecce timor Domini, ipsa est sapientia ;  et recedere a malo, intelligentia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר ׀ לָאָדָ֗ם הֵ֤ן יִרְאַ֣ת אֲ֭דֹנָי הִ֣יא חׇכְמָ֑ה וְס֖וּר מֵרָ֣ע בִּינָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to man: Behold the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom: and to depart from evil, is understanding.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sanoi ihmiselle: katso, Herran pelko on taito; ja karttaa pahaa on ymmärrys.
verses.king_james: And unto man he said, Behold, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom; and to depart from evil is understanding.
